mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, specifically a rise of 0.5 percentage points or more over three months, signals an impending economic slowdown or recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is declining while retail sales are simultaneously weakening, it indicates a broad-based contraction in both supply and demand, signaling a significant economic downturn that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If both INDPRO and RSAFS show 3-month declines, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained monetary tightening, indicated by rising federal funds rates, higher borrowing costs for corporations and consumers reduce economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25pp over 3 months during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced aggregate demand over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% over 6 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, signals weak underlying demand and potential deflationary pressures, which can lead to lower corporate pricing power and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month % change is below 1.0%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and mining activity, indicating strong supply-side capacity and economic expansion, which generally translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in real GDP growth, specifically a quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate above 3%, indicates strong economic expansion and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3.0%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 5-year rolling window.
